details.question: How much does the Business plan cost per month?
details.answer: The Business plan costs $299/month per workspace.
details.context:
<doc id="pricing.md" source="pricing.md">
## Add-ons

- **Additional storage**: $10/month per 100 GB above plan
- **Query overage**: $0.0005 per query above plan limit
- **Premium connectors** (Snowflake, Databricks): $99/month flat
- **Advanced AI features** (forecasting, anomaly detection): $199/month flat

## Free trial

All paid plans include a 14-day free trial. No credit card required to start. Trial usage is not metered against plan limits.
</doc>

<doc id="limits_and_quotas.md" source="limits_and_quotas.md">
## Query limits

| Limit | Free | Starter | Business | Enterprise |
|-------|------|---------|----------|------------|
| Queries per month | 10K | 100K | 1M | Custom |
| Query timeout | 1 min | 5 min | 30 min | 60 min |
| Result rows (inline) | 10K | 100K | 1M | 10M |
| Result rows (Parquet export) | 100K | 1M | 100M | Unlimited |
| Concurrent queries | 2 | 10 | 50 | Custom |

Excess queries on paid plans are billed at $0.0005 per query (you can set a hard cap).
</doc>

<doc id="pricing.md" source="pricing.md">
### Business — $299/month per workspace
- Up to 50 users
- 500 GB data storage
- 1M queries per month
- Priority email + chat support, 4h response SLA
- SSO via SAML 2.0
- 12-month query history
- Audit logs
- Custom branding

### Enterprise — custom pricing
- Unlimited users
- Custom storage tier
- Unlimited queries
- Dedicated support engineer, 1h response SLA
- SSO + SCIM provisioning
- HIPAA / SOC 2 Type II compliance
- Single-tenant deployment option
- 99.95% uptime SLA with credits
</doc>

<doc id="pricing.md" source="pricing.md">
## Plans

### Free
- Up to 3 users
- 5 GB data storage
- 10K queries per month
- Community support only
- Public dashboards only

### Starter — $49/month per workspace
- Up to 10 users
- 50 GB data storage
- 100K queries per month
- Email support, 24h response SLA
- Private dashboards
- 30-day query history
</doc>

<doc id="regions_and_sla.md" source="regions_and_sla.md">
## SLA details

### Uptime targets
- **Business plan**: 99.9% monthly (43 minutes max downtime per month)
- **Enterprise plan**: 99.95% monthly (22 minutes max downtime per month)

### What's covered
- Web app availability (app.northwind.cloud)
- API availability (api.northwind.cloud)
- Query engine response time under documented limits
- Authentication services
</doc>

details.question: What is the discount for annual billing?
details.answer: The discount for annual billing is 20%. This discount is applied when you pay upfront for the year, and it's available on all plans.
details.context:
<doc id="faq.md" source="faq.md">
## Pricing and billing

**Q: Can I change plans mid-cycle?**
A: Yes. Upgrades are pro-rated to the day. Downgrades take effect at next renewal.

**Q: Do you offer discounts?**
A: 20% discount for annual billing. Educational and non-profit discounts available — contact sales. Startup program offers 50% off Business plan for the first year for companies with <$2M ARR.
</doc>

<doc id="pricing.md" source="pricing.md">
## Billing and invoices

- Monthly billing on the day of signup
- Annual billing receives a 20% discount, paid upfront
- Invoices issued via Stripe; PDF available in account settings
- Accepted payment: credit card, ACH (US only), wire transfer (Enterprise only)
- Currency: USD by default, EUR and GBP available on Business and Enterprise plans
</doc>

<doc id="pricing.md" source="pricing.md">
# Pricing

Northwind Cloud offers four pricing tiers designed for teams from individual analysts to large enterprises. All prices are in USD and billed monthly unless noted. Annual billing receives a 20% discount.
</doc>

<doc id="billing.md" source="billing.md">
## Billing cycle

- **Monthly plans**: charged on the same day of each month as your initial signup. If signup date is the 31st, billing falls on the last day of months without a 31st.
- **Annual plans**: charged once per year, paid upfront. Receives a 20% discount versus monthly.
- **Pro-rated upgrades**: upgrading mid-cycle is pro-rated to the day. Downgrades take effect at next renewal.
</doc>

<doc id="billing.md" source="billing.md">
## Refunds and cancellations

- Cancel anytime from **Settings → Billing → Cancel subscription**
- Cancellation takes effect at the end of the current billing period
- No refunds for partial months on monthly plans
- Annual plans: pro-rated refund available within first 30 days only
- Enterprise contracts: refer to contract terms
</doc>

details.question: How many users are included in the Free plan?
details.answer: The Free plan includes up to 3 users.
details.context:

# Limits and Quotas

This page documents technical limits across plans. Limits are per workspace unless specified.

## User and access limits

| Limit | Free | Starter | Business | Enterprise |
|-------|------|---------|----------|------------|
| Users | 3 | 10 | 50 | Unlimited |
| Workspaces per organization | 1 | 3 | 10 | Unlimited |

<doc id="getting_started.md" source="getting_started.md">
## Step 1 — Create a workspace

1. Sign up at app.northwind.cloud with your work email
2. Verify your email via the link sent to your inbox (delivery takes 1–2 minutes)
3. Choose a workspace name and region (cannot be changed after creation)
4. Invite teammates by email (optional, can be done later)

The free plan gives you 14 days to evaluate paid features. After 14 days, the workspace reverts to Free tier limits unless upgraded.
</doc>

- **Advanced AI features** (forecasting, anomaly detection): $199/month flat

All paid plans include a 14-day free trial. No credit card required to start. Trial usage is not metered against plan limits.
</doc>

- 12-month query history

- Single-tenant deployment option

details.question: Which compliance certifications does Northwind have?
details.answer: Northwind Cloud has the following compliance certifications: SOC 2 Type II, ISO/IEC 27001:2022, HIPAA, GDPR, UK GDPR, and CCPA/CPRA.
details.ground_truth: SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, HIPAA, GDPR, CCPA
details.context:
<doc id="security.md" source="security.md">
# Security and Compliance

Northwind Cloud treats data security as a first-class concern. This document describes our security posture, certifications, and how customer data is protected.

## Certifications and compliance

- SOC 2 Type II audited annually by an independent firm
- ISO/IEC 27001:2022 certified
- HIPAA compliant infrastructure available on Enterprise plan
- GDPR and UK GDPR compliant; Data Processing Agreement available on request
- CCPA / CPRA compliant for California residents
</doc>

<doc id="policies.md" source="policies.md">
## Acceptable Use

What Northwind is not for:
- Storing personal medical records (use HIPAA-compliant Enterprise tier if you must)
- Real-time operational alerting (use PagerDuty / Datadog)
- Long-term archival storage (use S3 Glacier or equivalent)
- Mission-critical financial transactions (we are an analytics platform, not an OLTP database)

Violations may result in suspension. We will work with customers to migrate to appropriate solutions before terminating service.
</doc>

<doc id="onboarding.md" source="onboarding.md">
# Onboarding for Teams

This guide is for teams setting up Northwind Cloud at the organization level. For individual setup, see Getting Started.

## Choosing the right plan

For most teams of 5-50 users, the **Business plan** is the right starting point. It includes SSO, audit logs, and the SLA you'll need for production use.

Consider Enterprise if you need:
- HIPAA compliance
- Single-tenant deployment
- 99.95% uptime SLA
- Dedicated support engineer
- Custom contract terms
</doc>

<doc id="regions_and_sla.md" source="regions_and_sla.md">
## Available regions

Northwind Cloud is available in five geographic regions:

| Region code | Location | Compliance | Data residency |
|---|---|---|---|
| us-east-1 | Virginia, USA | SOC 2, HIPAA | US |
| us-west-2 | Oregon, USA | SOC 2, HIPAA | US |
| eu-west-1 | Ireland | SOC 2, GDPR | EU |
| eu-central-1 | Frankfurt | SOC 2, GDPR | EU |
| ap-southeast-1 | Singapore | SOC 2 | APAC |
</doc>

<doc id="features.md" source="features.md">
## What's not included

Northwind does not provide ETL transformation infrastructure (use Fivetran or Airbyte upstream), customer data platforms (use Segment or Rudderstack), or operational alerting (use PagerDuty or Datadog).
</doc>

details.question: Can I bring my own encryption key?
details.answer: Yes, you can bring your own encryption key, but only on the Enterprise plan, via AWS KMS, GCP Cloud KMS, or Azure Key Vault.
details.ground_truth: yes, on Enterprise plan via AWS KMS, GCP Cloud KMS, or Azure Key Vault
details.context:
<doc id="faq.md" source="faq.md">
**Q: Can I bring my own encryption key?**
A: Yes, on Enterprise plan via AWS KMS, GCP Cloud KMS, or Azure Key Vault.
</doc>

<doc id="security.md" source="security.md">
## Data encryption

- **In transit**: TLS 1.3 enforced on all API and web traffic. Older TLS versions are rejected at the load balancer.
- **At rest**: AES-256 server-side encryption on all storage layers. Customer can supply own KMS key on Enterprise plan (BYOK).
- **Backups**: encrypted daily snapshots retained 30 days, replicated cross-region.
</doc>

<doc id="api_reference.md" source="api_reference.md">
# API Reference

Northwind Cloud exposes a REST API for programmatic access to dashboards, queries, and data. The API is available on Starter plans and above.

## Authentication

All API requests require a Bearer token in the Authorization header:

```
Authorization: Bearer nw_live_<your_api_key>
```

API keys are created in **Settings → API keys**. Each key can be scoped to specific resources (read-only, write, admin) and given an expiry date.

## Base URL

```
https://api.northwind.cloud/v1
```
</doc>

<doc id="security.md" source="security.md">
## Authentication

- Email + password with mandatory 2FA for Admin and Editor roles
- Single sign-on via SAML 2.0 (Okta, Azure AD, Google Workspace, OneLogin) on Business and Enterprise plans
- SCIM 2.0 user provisioning on Enterprise plan
- API keys with configurable scopes and expiry
- OAuth 2.0 device flow for CLI
</doc>

<doc id="policies.md" source="policies.md">
### Cookies
- Essential cookies for authentication (required)
- Analytics cookies (Mixpanel, can be opted out)
- No third-party advertising cookies
</doc>

details.question: What TLS version is enforced?
details.answer: According to our security measures, TLS 1.3 is enforced on all API and web traffic. Older TLS versions are rejected at the load balancer. Additionally, we enforce a minimum of TLS 1.2 for database connections.
details.ground_truth: TLS 1.3
details.context:
<doc id="security.md" source="security.md">
## Data encryption

- **In transit**: TLS 1.3 enforced on all API and web traffic. Older TLS versions are rejected at the load balancer.
- **At rest**: AES-256 server-side encryption on all storage layers. Customer can supply own KMS key on Enterprise plan (BYOK).
- **Backups**: encrypted daily snapshots retained 30 days, replicated cross-region.
</doc>

<doc id="troubleshooting.md" source="troubleshooting.md">
### "Authentication failed"
- Confirm credentials by connecting from your local machine first
- For SSL-required databases, ensure SSL mode is set to `require` or `verify-ca` in connection settings
- Some databases (Oracle, MS SQL) require a TNS / instance name in addition to the host

### "SSL handshake failed"
- Northwind enforces TLS 1.2 minimum. If your database is behind an old proxy, upgrade the proxy.
- For self-signed certificates, upload the CA cert in connection settings and select "verify-ca"
</doc>

<doc id="integrations.md" source="integrations.md">
## Operational tools

### Identity providers (SSO)
Okta, Azure AD, Google Workspace, OneLogin, Auth0, generic SAML 2.0

### Monitoring
Datadog (metrics export), PagerDuty (alert routing), Opsgenie

### Version control
GitHub, GitLab — sync dashboards as code via the `nw-cli` tool
</doc>

<doc id="features.md" source="features.md">
### Collaboration
- Comments and threads on charts and dashboards
- Workspaces with role-based access control: Admin, Editor, Viewer
- Version history with one-click revert (last 90 days)
- Two-factor authentication required for Admin and Editor roles
</doc>

<doc id="policies.md" source="policies.md">
## Acceptable Use

What Northwind is not for:
- Storing personal medical records (use HIPAA-compliant Enterprise tier if you must)
- Real-time operational alerting (use PagerDuty / Datadog)
- Long-term archival storage (use S3 Glacier or equivalent)
- Mission-critical financial transactions (we are an analytics platform, not an OLTP database)

Violations may result in suspension. We will work with customers to migrate to appropriate solutions before terminating service.
</doc>

details.question: Where can I report security vulnerabilities?
details.answer: You can report security vulnerabilities to security@northwind.cloud. Northwind Cloud also operates a public bug bounty via HackerOne.
details.ground_truth: security@northwind.cloud or HackerOne bug bounty
details.context:
<doc id="security.md" source="security.md">
## Incident response

Security incidents are reported to affected customers within 24 hours of confirmation. Public security advisories are posted at status.northwind.cloud. Vulnerability reports can be submitted to security@northwind.cloud — we operate a public bug bounty via HackerOne.
</doc>

<doc id="faq.md" source="faq.md">
**Q: Do you have a SOC 2 report?**
A: Yes. SOC 2 Type II report available under NDA — request via security@northwind.cloud.
</doc>

<doc id="faq.md" source="faq.md">
## Data and security

**Q: Where is my data stored?**
A: In the region you selected at workspace creation: us-east-1, us-west-2, eu-west-1, eu-central-1, or ap-southeast-1. Data does not leave the selected region.

**Q: Do you train AI models on customer data?**
A: No. We never use customer data to train shared models. Per-customer fine-tuning is available on Enterprise plan with explicit opt-in.
</doc>

<doc id="security.md" source="security.md">
# Security and Compliance

Northwind Cloud treats data security as a first-class concern. This document describes our security posture, certifications, and how customer data is protected.

## Certifications and compliance

- SOC 2 Type II audited annually by an independent firm
- ISO/IEC 27001:2022 certified
- HIPAA compliant infrastructure available on Enterprise plan
- GDPR and UK GDPR compliant; Data Processing Agreement available on request
- CCPA / CPRA compliant for California residents
</doc>

<doc id="faq.md" source="faq.md">
**Q: Can I bring my own encryption key?**
A: Yes, on Enterprise plan via AWS KMS, GCP Cloud KMS, or Azure Key Vault.
</doc>

details.question: How do I create a workspace?
details.answer: To create a workspace, sign up at app.northwind.cloud with your work email, verify your email, choose a workspace name and region, and optionally invite teammates by email. Note that your chosen region cannot be changed after creation.
details.ground_truth: sign up at app.northwind.cloud, verify email, choose name and region
details.context:
<doc id="getting_started.md" source="getting_started.md">
## Step 1 — Create a workspace

1. Sign up at app.northwind.cloud with your work email
2. Verify your email via the link sent to your inbox (delivery takes 1–2 minutes)
3. Choose a workspace name and region (cannot be changed after creation)
4. Invite teammates by email (optional, can be done later)

The free plan gives you 14 days to evaluate paid features. After 14 days, the workspace reverts to Free tier limits unless upgraded.
</doc>

<doc id="regions_and_sla.md" source="regions_and_sla.md">
You select your region when creating a workspace. **Region cannot be changed after creation** — moving data requires creating a new workspace and re-syncing.
</doc>

<doc id="onboarding.md" source="onboarding.md">
## Permission model

### Roles (workspace-level)
- **Admin**: full access including billing, SSO, user management
- **Editor**: create and modify dashboards, run queries, manage data sources
- **Viewer**: read-only access to dashboards they have been granted

### Folders and granular permissions
- Dashboards live in folders
- Folder permissions: view-only, edit, manage (cascades to all dashboards inside)
- Specific dashboards can override folder permissions
</doc>

<doc id="features.md" source="features.md">
### Collaboration
- Comments and threads on charts and dashboards
- Workspaces with role-based access control: Admin, Editor, Viewer
- Version history with one-click revert (last 90 days)
- Two-factor authentication required for Admin and Editor roles
</doc>

<doc id="getting_started.md" source="getting_started.md">
# Getting Started

This guide walks through setting up your first Northwind Cloud workspace and connecting your first data source. Total time: about 15 minutes.
</doc>

details.question: How long does an initial sync usually take?
details.answer: The initial sync time depends on the data volume. A 100 GB warehouse typically syncs in 2-4 hours. Subsequent incremental syncs are generally faster, running in 1-15 minutes.
details.ground_truth: 2 to 4 hours for 100 GB warehouse
details.context:
<doc id="faq.md" source="faq.md">
## Technical

**Q: What's the maximum query result size?**
A: 100K rows for inline display. Larger results are exported to Parquet/CSV and downloaded via signed URL.

**Q: Can I use the platform offline?**
A: No. Northwind is cloud-only. Edge / on-prem deployments are not supported.

**Q: How long does an initial sync take?**
A: Depends on data volume. A 100 GB warehouse typically syncs in 2-4 hours. Subsequent incremental syncs run in 1-15 minutes.
</doc>

<doc id="getting_started.md" source="getting_started.md">
## Common first-time issues

- **Connection failed: timeout** — check that your database allows connections from Northwind IP ranges (listed in **Settings → Network**)
- **Empty tables** — verify the read-only user has SELECT permissions on the schema
- **Slow first sync** — initial sync can take longer than incremental syncs; subsequent updates are typically under 1 minute
</doc>

<doc id="limits_and_quotas.md" source="limits_and_quotas.md">
## Connection limits

| Limit | Free | Starter | Business | Enterprise |
|-------|------|---------|----------|------------|
| Active data sources | 1 | 5 | 50 | Unlimited |
| Sync frequency (minimum) | Daily | Hourly | 15 min | 1 min |
| Webhook endpoints | 1 | 10 | 100 | Unlimited |
</doc>

<doc id="getting_started.md" source="getting_started.md">
# Getting Started

This guide walks through setting up your first Northwind Cloud workspace and connecting your first data source. Total time: about 15 minutes.
</doc>

<doc id="getting_started.md" source="getting_started.md">
## Step 2 — Connect a data source

Northwind supports 60+ connectors. Most common path:

1. Go to **Data → Connections → Add new**
2. Select your source (PostgreSQL, MySQL, Snowflake, etc.)
3. Provide connection credentials. We recommend creating a read-only user on your database.
4. Test the connection — should succeed within 10 seconds
5. Select tables to sync and choose a refresh schedule (15min, 1h, 6h, daily)
</doc>

details.question: What is the API rate limit on the Business plan?
details.answer: The API rate limit for the Business plan is 600 requests per minute per key, with a burst limit of 1200 requests per key. Additionally, there's a limit of 6000 requests per minute per IP address.
details.ground_truth: 600 requests per minute
details.context:
<doc id="limits_and_quotas.md" source="limits_and_quotas.md">
## API rate limits

| Limit | Starter | Business | Enterprise |
|-------|---------|----------|------------|
| Requests per minute (per key) | 60 | 600 | Custom |
| Burst (per key) | 120 | 1200 | Custom |
| Requests per minute (per IP) | 600 | 6000 | Custom |
</doc>

<doc id="regions_and_sla.md" source="regions_and_sla.md">
## SLA details

### Uptime targets
- **Business plan**: 99.9% monthly (43 minutes max downtime per month)
- **Enterprise plan**: 99.95% monthly (22 minutes max downtime per month)

### What's covered
- Web app availability (app.northwind.cloud)
- API availability (api.northwind.cloud)
- Query engine response time under documented limits
- Authentication services
</doc>

<doc id="api_reference.md" source="api_reference.md">
## Rate limits

| Plan | Requests per minute | Burst |
|------|---------------------|-------|
| Starter | 60 | 120 |
| Business | 600 | 1200 |
| Enterprise | Custom | Custom |

Exceeding the limit returns HTTP 429 with a `Retry-After` header.
</doc>

<doc id="limits_and_quotas.md" source="limits_and_quotas.md">
## Query limits

| Limit | Free | Starter | Business | Enterprise |
|-------|------|---------|----------|------------|
| Queries per month | 10K | 100K | 1M | Custom |
| Query timeout | 1 min | 5 min | 30 min | 60 min |
| Result rows (inline) | 10K | 100K | 1M | 10M |
| Result rows (Parquet export) | 100K | 1M | 100M | Unlimited |
| Concurrent queries | 2 | 10 | 50 | Custom |

Excess queries on paid plans are billed at $0.0005 per query (you can set a hard cap).
</doc>

<doc id="limits_and_quotas.md" source="limits_and_quotas.md">
## Hitting a limit

When you approach a limit:
- 80% threshold: email notification to workspace admins
- 95% threshold: email + in-app banner
- 100% threshold: graceful degradation (queue queries, throttle API) plus option to upgrade or pay overage

To request a custom limit increase, contact support@northwind.cloud with your use case.
</doc>

details.question: How do I authenticate API requests?
details.answer: To authenticate API requests, you need to include a Bearer token in the Authorization header. The format is: `Authorization: Bearer nw_live_<your_api_key>`. You can create and manage API keys in the **Settings → API keys** section, where you can also scope the key to specific resources and set an expiry date.
details.ground_truth: Bearer token in Authorization header, API key from Settings
details.context:
<doc id="api_reference.md" source="api_reference.md">
# API Reference

Northwind Cloud exposes a REST API for programmatic access to dashboards, queries, and data. The API is available on Starter plans and above.

## Authentication

All API requests require a Bearer token in the Authorization header:

```
Authorization: Bearer nw_live_<your_api_key>
```

API keys are created in **Settings → API keys**. Each key can be scoped to specific resources (read-only, write, admin) and given an expiry date.

## Base URL

```
https://api.northwind.cloud/v1
```
</doc>

<doc id="troubleshooting.md" source="troubleshooting.md">
## Permission and access issues

### "I can't see a dashboard a teammate shared"
- Sharing is per-folder by default. Ask the teammate to verify folder permissions.
- Workspace admin can audit access via **Settings → Audit log**

### "API requests return 401"
- API keys can expire — check **Settings → API keys** for expiration date
- Confirm the key has appropriate scope (read vs write vs admin)
- Bearer prefix is required: `Authorization: Bearer nw_live_xxx`, not just the key
</doc>

<doc id="security.md" source="security.md">
## Authentication

- Email + password with mandatory 2FA for Admin and Editor roles
- Single sign-on via SAML 2.0 (Okta, Azure AD, Google Workspace, OneLogin) on Business and Enterprise plans
- SCIM 2.0 user provisioning on Enterprise plan
- API keys with configurable scopes and expiry
- OAuth 2.0 device flow for CLI
</doc>

<doc id="limits_and_quotas.md" source="limits_and_quotas.md">
## API rate limits

| Limit | Starter | Business | Enterprise |
|-------|---------|----------|------------|
| Requests per minute (per key) | 60 | 600 | Custom |
| Burst (per key) | 120 | 1200 | Custom |
| Requests per minute (per IP) | 600 | 6000 | Custom |
</doc>

<doc id="troubleshooting.md" source="troubleshooting.md">
### "Authentication failed"
- Confirm credentials by connecting from your local machine first
- For SSL-required databases, ensure SSL mode is set to `require` or `verify-ca` in connection settings
- Some databases (Oracle, MS SQL) require a TNS / instance name in addition to the host

### "SSL handshake failed"
- Northwind enforces TLS 1.2 minimum. If your database is behind an old proxy, upgrade the proxy.
- For self-signed certificates, upload the CA cert in connection settings and select "verify-ca"
</doc>

details.question: What is the API base URL?
details.answer: The API base URL is https://api.northwind.cloud/v1.
details.ground_truth: https://api.northwind.cloud/v1
details.context:
<doc id="api_reference.md" source="api_reference.md">
# API Reference

Northwind Cloud exposes a REST API for programmatic access to dashboards, queries, and data. The API is available on Starter plans and above.

## Authentication

All API requests require a Bearer token in the Authorization header:

```
Authorization: Bearer nw_live_<your_api_key>
```

API keys are created in **Settings → API keys**. Each key can be scoped to specific resources (read-only, write, admin) and given an expiry date.

## Base URL

```
https://api.northwind.cloud/v1
```
</doc>

<doc id="troubleshooting.md" source="troubleshooting.md">
## Permission and access issues

### "I can't see a dashboard a teammate shared"
- Sharing is per-folder by default. Ask the teammate to verify folder permissions.
- Workspace admin can audit access via **Settings → Audit log**

### "API requests return 401"
- API keys can expire — check **Settings → API keys** for expiration date
- Confirm the key has appropriate scope (read vs write vs admin)
- Bearer prefix is required: `Authorization: Bearer nw_live_xxx`, not just the key
</doc>

<doc id="security.md" source="security.md">
## Data encryption

- **In transit**: TLS 1.3 enforced on all API and web traffic. Older TLS versions are rejected at the load balancer.
- **At rest**: AES-256 server-side encryption on all storage layers. Customer can supply own KMS key on Enterprise plan (BYOK).
- **Backups**: encrypted daily snapshots retained 30 days, replicated cross-region.
</doc>

<doc id="limits_and_quotas.md" source="limits_and_quotas.md">
## API rate limits

| Limit | Starter | Business | Enterprise |
|-------|---------|----------|------------|
| Requests per minute (per key) | 60 | 600 | Custom |
| Burst (per key) | 120 | 1200 | Custom |
| Requests per minute (per IP) | 600 | 6000 | Custom |
</doc>

<doc id="api_reference.md" source="api_reference.md">
## Core endpoints

### Run a query

```
POST /queries/run
{
  "sql": "SELECT count(*) FROM events WHERE date > '2024-01-01'",
  "data_source_id": "ds_abc123",
  "max_rows": 10000
}
```

Returns query results as JSON or, for large results, a signed URL to a Parquet file.

### List dashboards

```
GET /dashboards?workspace_id=ws_xyz789&limit=50
```

### Get dashboard data

```
GET /dashboards/{dashboard_id}/data
```

Returns all chart data for a dashboard in a single response.
</doc>

details.question: Which Python SDK does Northwind provide?
details.answer: The Northwind Cloud Python SDK can be installed using `pip install northwind-cloud`.
details.ground_truth: pip install northwind-cloud
details.context:
<doc id="api_reference.md" source="api_reference.md">
## SDKs

Official client libraries:
- Python: `pip install northwind-cloud`
- Node.js: `npm install @northwind/cloud`
- Go: `go get github.com/northwind/cloud-go`

## Webhooks

Northwind can POST to your endpoint when events occur (sync completed, query failed, alert triggered). Configure in **Settings → Webhooks**. Payload is signed with HMAC SHA-256; verify the `X-Northwind-Signature` header.
</doc>

<doc id="integrations.md" source="integrations.md">
## Custom integrations

If your tool is not on the list, you can:

1. Use the **Generic JDBC connector** for any database with a JDBC driver
2. Use the **HTTP Pull connector** for any REST API returning JSON
3. Write a custom Python connector using the SDK at github.com/northwind/connector-sdk
</doc>

<doc id="features.md" source="features.md">
## What's not included

Northwind does not provide ETL transformation infrastructure (use Fivetran or Airbyte upstream), customer data platforms (use Segment or Rudderstack), or operational alerting (use PagerDuty or Datadog).
</doc>

<doc id="api_reference.md" source="api_reference.md">
# API Reference

Northwind Cloud exposes a REST API for programmatic access to dashboards, queries, and data. The API is available on Starter plans and above.

## Authentication

All API requests require a Bearer token in the Authorization header:

```
Authorization: Bearer nw_live_<your_api_key>
```

API keys are created in **Settings → API keys**. Each key can be scoped to specific resources (read-only, write, admin) and given an expiry date.

## Base URL

```
https://api.northwind.cloud/v1
```
</doc>

<doc id="faq.md" source="faq.md">
## General

**Q: What does Northwind Cloud do?**
A: It's a unified analytics platform combining data warehousing, BI dashboards, and AI-driven insights. Teams use it to consolidate data from multiple sources, build interactive dashboards, and surface insights automatically.

**Q: Who is it for?**
A: Product analytics, BI, and data teams at companies from 10 to 10,000 employees. Common roles: data analyst, analytics engineer, product manager, founder.
</doc>

details.question: Does Northwind support Snowflake?
details.answer: Yes, Northwind supports Snowflake. According to the "getting_started.md" document, Snowflake is listed as one of the data sources that can be connected to Northwind.
details.ground_truth: yes, native connector available
details.context:
<doc id="getting_started.md" source="getting_started.md">
## Step 2 — Connect a data source

Northwind supports 60+ connectors. Most common path:

1. Go to **Data → Connections → Add new**
2. Select your source (PostgreSQL, MySQL, Snowflake, etc.)
3. Provide connection credentials. We recommend creating a read-only user on your database.
4. Test the connection — should succeed within 10 seconds
5. Select tables to sync and choose a refresh schedule (15min, 1h, 6h, daily)
</doc>

<doc id="features.md" source="features.md">
## What's not included

Northwind does not provide ETL transformation infrastructure (use Fivetran or Airbyte upstream), customer data platforms (use Segment or Rudderstack), or operational alerting (use PagerDuty or Datadog).
</doc>

<doc id="troubleshooting.md" source="troubleshooting.md">
# Troubleshooting

Common issues and how to fix them. If your problem isn't listed here, contact support@northwind.cloud.
</doc>

<doc id="migration_guide.md" source="migration_guide.md">
# Migration Guide

This guide helps teams moving to Northwind Cloud from existing analytics platforms. We support migrations from Tableau, Looker, Metabase, Mode, and homegrown solutions.
</doc>

<doc id="faq.md" source="faq.md">
## Technical

**Q: What's the maximum query result size?**
A: 100K rows for inline display. Larger results are exported to Parquet/CSV and downloaded via signed URL.

**Q: Can I use the platform offline?**
A: No. Northwind is cloud-only. Edge / on-prem deployments are not supported.

**Q: How long does an initial sync take?**
A: Depends on data volume. A 100 GB warehouse typically syncs in 2-4 hours. Subsequent incremental syncs run in 1-15 minutes.
</doc>

details.question: Which CRMs are supported?
details.answer: The supported CRMs are Salesforce, HubSpot, Pipedrive, and Zoho.

<doc id="integrations.md" source="integrations.md">
### SaaS applications
- **CRM**: Salesforce, HubSpot, Pipedrive, Zoho
- **Marketing**: Marketo, Mailchimp, ActiveCampaign, Klaviyo
- **Product analytics**: Mixpanel, Amplitude, Segment
- **Payments**: Stripe, Braintree, Adyen
- **Support**: Zendesk, Intercom, Freshdesk

### Streaming
Apache Kafka, AWS Kinesis, Google Pub/Sub
</doc>

For files (CSV, JSON, Parquet), use **Data → Upload** and drag-and-drop. Files up to 10 GB are supported.

<doc id="faq.md" source="faq.md">
## Technical

**Q: What's the maximum query result size?**
A: 100K rows for inline display. Larger results are exported to Parquet/CSV and downloaded via signed URL.

**Q: Can I use the platform offline?**
A: No. Northwind is cloud-only. Edge / on-prem deployments are not supported.

**Q: How long does an initial sync take?**
A: Depends on data volume. A 100 GB warehouse typically syncs in 2-4 hours. Subsequent incremental syncs run in 1-15 minutes.

<doc id="features.md" source="features.md">
## Core capabilities

### Data ingestion
- 60+ pre-built connectors: PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, S3, GCS, Stripe, Salesforce, HubSpot, Segment
- Real-time streaming via Kafka and Kinesis

</doc>

details.question: Can I send dashboard reports to Slack?
details.answer: Yes, you can send dashboard reports to Slack. According to the features and integrations available, Northwind Cloud supports sending scheduled reports to Slack, in addition to email and other destinations.
details.ground_truth: yes, scheduled reports and alerts to Slack
details.context:
<doc id="getting_started.md" source="getting_started.md">
## Step 4 — Set up scheduled reports (optional)

1. Open any dashboard
2. Click **Schedule → New schedule**
3. Choose recipients (email addresses or Slack channels)
4. Set frequency (daily, weekly, monthly)
5. Optionally restrict to filtered views
</doc>

<doc id="features.md" source="features.md">
### Dashboards and visualizations
- 25+ chart types including geo-maps, funnels, cohort analysis, Sankey
- Interactive filters and drill-down on any chart
- Embedding via signed iframes for customer-facing dashboards
- Scheduled email and Slack reports
- Public sharing with read-only links

### AI and machine learning
- Natural-language to SQL via integrated LLM
- Forecasting (ARIMA, Prophet) on any metric
- Anomaly detection with configurable sensitivity
- Automated insight discovery on dashboards
</doc>

<doc id="integrations.md" source="integrations.md">
## Output destinations

- **Slack**: scheduled reports, alerts, dashboard links
- **Microsoft Teams**: same as Slack
- **Email**: scheduled PDF or PNG exports
- **Webhooks**: any HTTP endpoint
- **Reverse ETL**: write transformed data back to Salesforce, HubSpot, or any database
</doc>

<doc id="onboarding.md" source="onboarding.md">
## Data governance

### Tagging
- Tag data sources with sensitivity levels: Public, Internal, Confidential, Restricted
- Tag flows to dashboards using those sources
- Set policies preventing low-clearance users from creating dashboards on Confidential data

### Audit logs
- Every query, dashboard view, and admin action is logged
- Logs visible in **Settings → Audit log** (Admin only)
- Logs exportable to S3 or sent to Datadog/Splunk in real time
- Retention: 12 months on Business, 7 years on Enterprise
</doc>

<doc id="troubleshooting.md" source="troubleshooting.md">
## Getting help

For support faster than email:
- Business plan: live chat in the bottom-right of the app, M-F 9am-6pm ET
- Enterprise: dedicated Slack channel with your assigned support engineer
- Status page: status.northwind.cloud (public)
</doc>

details.question: What payment methods do you accept?
details.answer: We accept the following payment methods: Credit card (Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Discover), ACH direct debit (US customers), SEPA direct debit (EU customers on Business and above plans), and Wire transfer (Enterprise only with a $5K minimum invoice). We also accept Purchase orders for Enterprise customers on approved credit.
details.ground_truth: credit card, ACH (US), SEPA (EU), wire transfer (Enterprise), purchase orders (Enterprise)
details.context:
<doc id="billing.md" source="billing.md">
## Accepted payment methods

| Method | Plans |
|--------|-------|
| Credit card (Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Discover) | All paid plans |
| ACH direct debit | US customers, all paid plans |
| SEPA direct debit | EU customers, Business and above |
| Wire transfer | Enterprise only, $5K minimum invoice |
| Purchase orders | Enterprise only, on approved credit |

We do not accept cryptocurrency, PayPal, or international money orders.
</doc>

<doc id="billing.md" source="billing.md">
# Billing and Payments

This document covers how billing works at Northwind Cloud, accepted payment methods, and how to manage your subscription.
</doc>

<doc id="pricing.md" source="pricing.md">
## Billing and invoices

- Monthly billing on the day of signup
- Annual billing receives a 20% discount, paid upfront
- Invoices issued via Stripe; PDF available in account settings
- Accepted payment: credit card, ACH (US only), wire transfer (Enterprise only)
- Currency: USD by default, EUR and GBP available on Business and Enterprise plans
</doc>

<doc id="billing.md" source="billing.md">
## Failed payments

- Card decline: we retry on day 3, 5, and 7 after failure
- After 3 failed retries, workspace access is suspended (read-only mode)
- After 14 days of suspension, workspace data is archived
- After 90 days of archival, data is permanently deleted

To restore service, update payment method in billing settings and contact support@northwind.cloud if needed.
</doc>

<doc id="faq.md" source="faq.md">
## Pricing and billing

**Q: Can I change plans mid-cycle?**
A: Yes. Upgrades are pro-rated to the day. Downgrades take effect at next renewal.

**Q: Do you offer discounts?**
A: 20% discount for annual billing. Educational and non-profit discounts available — contact sales. Startup program offers 50% off Business plan for the first year for companies with <$2M ARR.
</doc>

details.question: Do you accept cryptocurrency?
details.answer: No, we do not accept cryptocurrency as a payment method. According to our accepted payment methods, we accept credit cards, ACH direct debit, SEPA direct debit, wire transfer, and purchase orders, but cryptocurrency is not listed as an accepted method.
details.ground_truth: no
details.context:
<doc id="billing.md" source="billing.md">
## Accepted payment methods

| Method | Plans |
|--------|-------|
| Credit card (Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Discover) | All paid plans |
| ACH direct debit | US customers, all paid plans |
| SEPA direct debit | EU customers, Business and above |
| Wire transfer | Enterprise only, $5K minimum invoice |
| Purchase orders | Enterprise only, on approved credit |

We do not accept cryptocurrency, PayPal, or international money orders.
</doc>

<doc id="policies.md" source="policies.md">
## Terms of Service

By using Northwind Cloud you agree to:
- Not reverse-engineer the platform or attempt to bypass rate limits
- Not store illegal content, malware, or violate third-party copyrights
- Not use the platform for cryptocurrency mining, password cracking, or DDoS
- Comply with applicable export control laws (US OFAC, EU sanctions)

We reserve the right to suspend accounts violating these terms with 24h written notice (immediate suspension for severe violations).
</doc>

<doc id="faq.md" source="faq.md">
**Q: Can I bring my own encryption key?**
A: Yes, on Enterprise plan via AWS KMS, GCP Cloud KMS, or Azure Key Vault.
</doc>

<doc id="security.md" source="security.md">
## Data encryption

- **In transit**: TLS 1.3 enforced on all API and web traffic. Older TLS versions are rejected at the load balancer.
- **At rest**: AES-256 server-side encryption on all storage layers. Customer can supply own KMS key on Enterprise plan (BYOK).
- **Backups**: encrypted daily snapshots retained 30 days, replicated cross-region.
</doc>

<doc id="faq.md" source="faq.md">
**Q: Do you have a SOC 2 report?**
A: Yes. SOC 2 Type II report available under NDA — request via security@northwind.cloud.
</doc>

details.question: What happens if my payment fails?
details.answer: If your payment fails, we will retry the payment on day 3, 5, and 7 after the failure. If all retries fail, your workspace access will be suspended after 3 failed retries, and your data will be archived after 14 days of suspension. To restore service, you can update your payment method in billing settings and contact support@northwind.cloud if needed.
details.ground_truth: retried on day 3, 5, 7; suspended after 3 failed retries; archived after 14 days
details.context:
<doc id="billing.md" source="billing.md">
## Failed payments

- Card decline: we retry on day 3, 5, and 7 after failure
- After 3 failed retries, workspace access is suspended (read-only mode)
- After 14 days of suspension, workspace data is archived
- After 90 days of archival, data is permanently deleted

To restore service, update payment method in billing settings and contact support@northwind.cloud if needed.
</doc>

<doc id="faq.md" source="faq.md">
**Q: What happens if I exceed my query limit?**
A: Queries continue to work, billed at $0.0005 per query above plan. You can set a hard cap in billing settings to prevent overage.
</doc>

<doc id="billing.md" source="billing.md">
# Billing and Payments

This document covers how billing works at Northwind Cloud, accepted payment methods, and how to manage your subscription.
</doc>

<doc id="troubleshooting.md" source="troubleshooting.md">
# Troubleshooting

Common issues and how to fix them. If your problem isn't listed here, contact support@northwind.cloud.
</doc>

<doc id="billing.md" source="billing.md">
## Billing cycle

- **Monthly plans**: charged on the same day of each month as your initial signup. If signup date is the 31st, billing falls on the last day of months without a 31st.
- **Annual plans**: charged once per year, paid upfront. Receives a 20% discount versus monthly.
- **Pro-rated upgrades**: upgrading mid-cycle is pro-rated to the day. Downgrades take effect at next renewal.
</doc>

details.question: Can I get a refund on annual plans?
details.answer: For annual plans, a pro-rated refund is available, but only within the first 30 days of your subscription.
details.ground_truth: pro-rated refund within first 30 days only
details.context:
<doc id="billing.md" source="billing.md">
## Refunds and cancellations

- Cancel anytime from **Settings → Billing → Cancel subscription**
- Cancellation takes effect at the end of the current billing period
- No refunds for partial months on monthly plans
- Annual plans: pro-rated refund available within first 30 days only
- Enterprise contracts: refer to contract terms
</doc>

<doc id="faq.md" source="faq.md">

</doc>

## Billing cycle

- **Monthly plans**: charged on the same day of each month as your initial signup. If signup date is the 31st, billing falls on the last day of months without a 31st.
- **Annual plans**: charged once per year, paid upfront. Receives a 20% discount versus monthly.
- **Pro-rated upgrades**: upgrading mid-cycle is pro-rated to the day. Downgrades take effect at next renewal.

# Pricing

Northwind Cloud offers four pricing tiers designed for teams from individual analysts to large enterprises. All prices are in USD and billed monthly unless noted. Annual billing receives a 20% discount.
</doc>

details.question: Why am I getting a connection timeout error?
details.answer: To resolve the connection timeout error, verify that Northwind IP ranges are allowed in your firewall and check if your database accepts external connections. You can find the Northwind IP ranges in **Settings → Network**. If you're using VPC peering, confirm the peering connection is in an `active` state. If issues persist, consider reaching out to support@northwind.cloud for further assistance.
details.ground_truth: check Northwind IP ranges in firewall, database accepts external connections
details.context:
<doc id="troubleshooting.md" source="troubleshooting.md">
## Connection issues

### "Connection failed: timeout"
- Verify Northwind IP ranges are allowed in your firewall (listed in **Settings → Network**)
- Check the database accepts external connections (often disabled by default in cloud providers)
- For VPC peering customers: confirm the peering connection is in `active` state
</doc>

<doc id="getting_started.md" source="getting_started.md">
## Common first-time issues

- **Connection failed: timeout** — check that your database allows connections from Northwind IP ranges (listed in **Settings → Network**)
- **Empty tables** — verify the read-only user has SELECT permissions on the schema
- **Slow first sync** — initial sync can take longer than incremental syncs; subsequent updates are typically under 1 minute
</doc>

<doc id="troubleshooting.md" source="troubleshooting.md">
## Query and dashboard issues

### "Query timed out"
- Default timeout is 5 minutes. Increase to 30 minutes in **Settings → Query → Timeout**.
- For long-running queries, consider materializing intermediate results into a scheduled table.
- Add appropriate indexes to source tables (Northwind will warn if it detects a sequential scan over 1M rows)
</doc>

<doc id="troubleshooting.md" source="troubleshooting.md">
### "Authentication failed"
- Confirm credentials by connecting from your local machine first
- For SSL-required databases, ensure SSL mode is set to `require` or `verify-ca` in connection settings
- Some databases (Oracle, MS SQL) require a TNS / instance name in addition to the host

### "SSL handshake failed"
- Northwind enforces TLS 1.2 minimum. If your database is behind an old proxy, upgrade the proxy.
- For self-signed certificates, upload the CA cert in connection settings and select "verify-ca"
</doc>

<doc id="getting_started.md" source="getting_started.md">
## Step 2 — Connect a data source

Northwind supports 60+ connectors. Most common path:

1. Go to **Data → Connections → Add new**
2. Select your source (PostgreSQL, MySQL, Snowflake, etc.)
3. Provide connection credentials. We recommend creating a read-only user on your database.
4. Test the connection — should succeed within 10 seconds
5. Select tables to sync and choose a refresh schedule (15min, 1h, 6h, daily)
</doc>

details.question: How can I make a slow dashboard faster?
details.answer: To improve a slow dashboard's performance, you can check the **Dashboard → Performance** section to see per-chart load times. Additionally, enabling **Auto-cache** on the dashboard can help by caching results for up to 24 hours. You can also optimize the underlying queries by replacing SELECT * with explicit column lists. If you need further assistance, consider contacting support@northwind.cloud.
details.ground_truth: enable Auto-cache, replace SELECT * with explicit columns, check Performance tab
details.context:
<doc id="troubleshooting.md" source="troubleshooting.md">
### "Dashboard loading is slow"
- Check **Dashboard → Performance** — shows per-chart load times
- Enable **Auto-cache** on the dashboard to cache results for up to 24 hours
- Replace SELECT * with explicit column lists in underlying queries
</doc>

<doc id="getting_started.md" source="getting_started.md">
## Step 3 — Build your first dashboard

1. Go to **Dashboards → New dashboard**
2. Click **Add chart** and select a data source from Step 2
3. Use the visual query builder or write SQL directly
4. Choose a chart type (bar, line, table, etc.)
5. Apply filters, group-by, and aggregations as needed
6. Save and share with teammates via the share button
</doc>

<doc id="troubleshooting.md" source="troubleshooting.md">
## Query and dashboard issues

### "Query timed out"
- Default timeout is 5 minutes. Increase to 30 minutes in **Settings → Query → Timeout**.
- For long-running queries, consider materializing intermediate results into a scheduled table.
- Add appropriate indexes to source tables (Northwind will warn if it detects a sequential scan over 1M rows)
</doc>

<doc id="migration_guide.md" source="migration_guide.md">
## Post-migration

### Validation
- Compare 5-10 critical dashboards side-by-side for 1 week
- Confirm aggregated numbers match (within rounding)
- Investigate any discrepancies — usually GROUP BY or filter differences

### Cutover
- Update DNS / bookmark links to new dashboards
- Add a deprecation banner on legacy dashboards 30 days before retirement
- Archive (don't delete) old dashboards for 90 days as fallback
</doc>

<doc id="getting_started.md" source="getting_started.md">
## Step 4 — Set up scheduled reports (optional)

1. Open any dashboard
2. Click **Schedule → New schedule**
3. Choose recipients (email addresses or Slack channels)
4. Set frequency (daily, weekly, monthly)
5. Optionally restrict to filtered views
</doc>

details.question: What is the uptime SLA on Enterprise?
details.answer: The uptime SLA for the Enterprise plan is 99.95% monthly, which translates to a maximum of 22 minutes of downtime per month.
details.context:
<doc id="regions_and_sla.md" source="regions_and_sla.md">
## SLA details

### Uptime targets
- **Business plan**: 99.9% monthly (43 minutes max downtime per month)
- **Enterprise plan**: 99.95% monthly (22 minutes max downtime per month)

### What's covered
- Web app availability (app.northwind.cloud)
- API availability (api.northwind.cloud)
- Query engine response time under documented limits
- Authentication services
</doc>

<doc id="policies.md" source="policies.md">
## Service Level Agreement (SLA)

| Plan | Uptime SLA | Support response | Credit policy |
|------|-----------|-----------------|---------------|
| Free / Starter | None | 24-48h | None |
| Business | 99.9% | 4h business hours | 10% / 25% / 50% credit per tier of breach |
| Enterprise | 99.95% | 1h 24/7 | Custom per contract |

Uptime is measured against the public status page at status.northwind.cloud. Scheduled maintenance is excluded from SLA calculations and announced 7+ days in advance.
</doc>

<doc id="onboarding.md" source="onboarding.md">
# Onboarding for Teams

This guide is for teams setting up Northwind Cloud at the organization level. For individual setup, see Getting Started.

## Choosing the right plan

For most teams of 5-50 users, the **Business plan** is the right starting point. It includes SSO, audit logs, and the SLA you'll need for production use.

Consider Enterprise if you need:
- HIPAA compliance
- Single-tenant deployment
- 99.95% uptime SLA
- Dedicated support engineer
- Custom contract terms
</doc>

<doc id="pricing.md" source="pricing.md">
### Business — $299/month per workspace
- Up to 50 users
- 500 GB data storage
- 1M queries per month
- Priority email + chat support, 4h response SLA
- SSO via SAML 2.0
- 12-month query history
- Audit logs
- Custom branding

### Enterprise — custom pricing
- Unlimited users
- Custom storage tier
- Unlimited queries
- Dedicated support engineer, 1h response SLA
- SSO + SCIM provisioning
- HIPAA / SOC 2 Type II compliance
- Single-tenant deployment option
- 99.95% uptime SLA with credits
</doc>

<doc id="regions_and_sla.md" source="regions_and_sla.md">
### SLA credits

If we fall below the SLA in a month, you receive credits applied to next invoice:

| Uptime | Credit |
|--------|--------|
| 99.0% – 99.9% | 10% |
| 95.0% – 99.0% | 25% |
| Below 95.0% | 50% |

To claim credits, file a ticket within 30 days of the affected month. Credits are not paid in cash and do not exceed your monthly invoice.
</doc>

details.question: Which regions does Northwind support?
details.answer:
Northwind Cloud is available in five geographic regions: 

1. us-east-1 (Virginia, USA)
2. us-west-2 (Oregon, USA)
3. eu-west-1 (Ireland)
4. eu-central-1 (Frankfurt)
5. ap-southeast-1 (Singapore)
details.ground_truth: us-east-1, us-west-2, eu-west-1, eu-central-1, ap-southeast-1
details.context:
<doc id="regions_and_sla.md" source="regions_and_sla.md">
## Available regions

Northwind Cloud is available in five geographic regions:

| Region code | Location | Compliance | Data residency |
|---|---|---|---|
| us-east-1 | Virginia, USA | SOC 2, HIPAA | US |
| us-west-2 | Oregon, USA | SOC 2, HIPAA | US |
| eu-west-1 | Ireland | SOC 2, GDPR | EU |
| eu-central-1 | Frankfurt | SOC 2, GDPR | EU |
| ap-southeast-1 | Singapore | SOC 2 | APAC |
</doc>

<doc id="migration_guide.md" source="migration_guide.md">
# Migration Guide

This guide helps teams moving to Northwind Cloud from existing analytics platforms. We support migrations from Tableau, Looker, Metabase, Mode, and homegrown solutions.
</doc>

<doc id="features.md" source="features.md">
## What's not included

Northwind does not provide ETL transformation infrastructure (use Fivetran or Airbyte upstream), customer data platforms (use Segment or Rudderstack), or operational alerting (use PagerDuty or Datadog).
</doc>

<doc id="features.md" source="features.md">
# Features

Northwind Cloud is a unified analytics platform that combines data warehousing, BI dashboards, and AI-driven insights for product teams.
</doc>

<doc id="integrations.md" source="integrations.md">
# Integrations

Northwind Cloud integrates with the tools your team already uses. Integrations are organized into three categories: data sources, output destinations, and operational tools.
</doc>

details.question: Can I move my workspace to a different region?
details.answer: No, you cannot change the region of your workspace after creation. According to our documentation, "Region cannot be changed after creation" and moving data would require creating a new workspace and re-syncing. If you need further assistance, please contact support@northwind.cloud.
details.ground_truth: no, region cannot be changed after creation
details.context:
<doc id="regions_and_sla.md" source="regions_and_sla.md">
You select your region when creating a workspace. **Region cannot be changed after creation** — moving data requires creating a new workspace and re-syncing.
</doc>

<doc id="security.md" source="security.md">
## Network security

- VPC peering and PrivateLink available on Enterprise
- IP allowlisting per workspace on Business and above
- Web Application Firewall in front of all customer-facing endpoints
- DDoS mitigation via CloudFront

## Data residency

Customer data stays in the region selected at workspace creation. We do not move data across regions without explicit customer authorization. EU customers can pin to eu-west-1 or eu-central-1 for full GDPR compliance.
</doc>

<doc id="faq.md" source="faq.md">
## Data and security

**Q: Where is my data stored?**
A: In the region you selected at workspace creation: us-east-1, us-west-2, eu-west-1, eu-central-1, or ap-southeast-1. Data does not leave the selected region.

**Q: Do you train AI models on customer data?**
A: No. We never use customer data to train shared models. Per-customer fine-tuning is available on Enterprise plan with explicit opt-in.
</doc>

<doc id="getting_started.md" source="getting_started.md">
## Step 1 — Create a workspace

1. Sign up at app.northwind.cloud with your work email
2. Verify your email via the link sent to your inbox (delivery takes 1–2 minutes)
3. Choose a workspace name and region (cannot be changed after creation)
4. Invite teammates by email (optional, can be done later)

The free plan gives you 14 days to evaluate paid features. After 14 days, the workspace reverts to Free tier limits unless upgraded.
</doc>

<doc id="regions_and_sla.md" source="regions_and_sla.md">
# Regions and SLA
</doc>

details.question: Do you train AI models on customer data?
details.answer: No, we never use customer data to train shared AI models. However, per-customer fine-tuning is available on the Enterprise plan with explicit opt-in.
details.ground_truth: no, customer data is never used to train shared models
details.context:
<doc id="faq.md" source="faq.md">
## Data and security

**Q: Where is my data stored?**
A: In the region you selected at workspace creation: us-east-1, us-west-2, eu-west-1, eu-central-1, or ap-southeast-1. Data does not leave the selected region.

**Q: Do you train AI models on customer data?**
A: No. We never use customer data to train shared models. Per-customer fine-tuning is available on Enterprise plan with explicit opt-in.
</doc>

<doc id="policies.md" source="policies.md">
## AI Usage Policy

- Customer data is **never** used to train shared AI models
- Embeddings are generated locally per workspace and stored encrypted
- LLM calls (e.g., natural-language to SQL) send only the user's query plus relevant schema, not data values
- Per-customer fine-tuning available on Enterprise with explicit opt-in and DPA amendment
</doc>

<doc id="policies.md" source="policies.md">
## Privacy Policy

### What we collect
- Account info: email, name, organization
- Usage data: queries run, dashboards viewed, features used (aggregated for product improvement)
- Customer data: only what you explicitly upload or connect (we are a Data Processor under GDPR)

### What we don't collect
- We don't track you across other websites
- We don't sell or share data with advertisers
- We don't use customer data to train AI models (see also AI Policy below)
</doc>

<doc id="features.md" source="features.md">
### Dashboards and visualizations
- 25+ chart types including geo-maps, funnels, cohort analysis, Sankey
- Interactive filters and drill-down on any chart
- Embedding via signed iframes for customer-facing dashboards
- Scheduled email and Slack reports
- Public sharing with read-only links

### AI and machine learning
- Natural-language to SQL via integrated LLM
- Forecasting (ARIMA, Prophet) on any metric
- Anomaly detection with configurable sensitivity
- Automated insight discovery on dashboards
</doc>

<doc id="security.md" source="security.md">
# Security and Compliance

Northwind Cloud treats data security as a first-class concern. This document describes our security posture, certifications, and how customer data is protected.

## Certifications and compliance

- SOC 2 Type II audited annually by an independent firm
- ISO/IEC 27001:2022 certified
- HIPAA compliant infrastructure available on Enterprise plan
- GDPR and UK GDPR compliant; Data Processing Agreement available on request
- CCPA / CPRA compliant for California residents
</doc>

details.question: How long are audit logs retained?
details.answer: Audit logs are retained for 12 months on the Business plan and 7 years on the Enterprise plan.
details.ground_truth: 12 months on Business, 7 years on Enterprise
details.context:
<doc id="policies.md" source="policies.md">
## Data Retention

- Active workspaces: data retained as long as subscription is active
- Cancelled workspaces: data retained 30 days, then permanently deleted
- Deleted accounts: backups retained 90 days for disaster recovery, then purged
- Audit logs: retained 12 months on Business, 7 years on Enterprise (regulatory requirement)
</doc>

<doc id="limits_and_quotas.md" source="limits_and_quotas.md">
## Other notable limits

- Maximum query response size: 100 MB inline (larger via Parquet)
- Maximum dashboard refresh frequency: 1 minute on Enterprise, 15 min on Business
- Maximum scheduled report frequency: every 15 minutes
- Maximum recipients per scheduled report: 50
- Audit log retention: 12 months (Business) / 7 years (Enterprise)
- Backup retention: 30 days (all paid plans)
</doc>

<doc id="onboarding.md" source="onboarding.md">
## Data governance

### Tagging
- Tag data sources with sensitivity levels: Public, Internal, Confidential, Restricted
- Tag flows to dashboards using those sources
- Set policies preventing low-clearance users from creating dashboards on Confidential data

### Audit logs
- Every query, dashboard view, and admin action is logged
- Logs visible in **Settings → Audit log** (Admin only)
- Logs exportable to S3 or sent to Datadog/Splunk in real time
- Retention: 12 months on Business, 7 years on Enterprise
</doc>

<doc id="pricing.md" source="pricing.md">
### Business — $299/month per workspace
- Up to 50 users
- 500 GB data storage
- 1M queries per month
- Priority email + chat support, 4h response SLA
- SSO via SAML 2.0
- 12-month query history
- Audit logs
- Custom branding

### Enterprise — custom pricing
- Unlimited users
- Custom storage tier
- Unlimited queries
- Dedicated support engineer, 1h response SLA
- SSO + SCIM provisioning
- HIPAA / SOC 2 Type II compliance
- Single-tenant deployment option
- 99.95% uptime SLA with credits
</doc>

<doc id="troubleshooting.md" source="troubleshooting.md">
## Permission and access issues

### "I can't see a dashboard a teammate shared"
- Sharing is per-folder by default. Ask the teammate to verify folder permissions.
- Workspace admin can audit access via **Settings → Audit log**

### "API requests return 401"
- API keys can expire — check **Settings → API keys** for expiration date
- Confirm the key has appropriate scope (read vs write vs admin)
- Bearer prefix is required: `Authorization: Bearer nw_live_xxx`, not just the key
</doc>

details.question: Can I import dashboards from Tableau?
details.answer: Yes, you can import dashboards from Tableau. Northwind Cloud provides a tool called `nw-tableau-importer` to bulk-import .twbx files. However, please note that some features may require manual rework after import. It's recommended to review the imported content for accuracy. If you need further assistance, you can contact support@northwind.cloud.
details.ground_truth: yes, via nw-tableau-importer CLI for .twbx files
details.context:
<doc id="faq.md" source="faq.md">
**Q: How does it compare to Looker / Tableau / Metabase?**
A: We focus on the full pipeline (ingestion + warehouse + dashboards + AI) in one product. Tableau and Looker are dashboard-only and require separate ETL and warehouse. Metabase is similar but lacks AI features and managed warehouse.
</doc>

<doc id="migration_guide.md" source="migration_guide.md">
## From Tableau

### What transfers cleanly
- SQL queries and data models (export from Tableau, paste into Northwind)
- Calculated fields (most syntax is similar)
- Standard chart types (bar, line, scatter, pie, table)
- Filters and parameters

### What requires manual rework
- Tableau-specific features: sets, bins, table calculations
- Custom Tableau extensions (no equivalent)
- LOD expressions (require rewriting in SQL)
- Story / dashboard actions (use Northwind's drill-down instead)
</doc>

<doc id="migration_guide.md" source="migration_guide.md">
### Tooling
- Use `nw-tableau-importer` CLI to bulk-import .twbx files (best-effort, manual review needed)
- Available at github.com/northwind/migration-tools
</doc>

<doc id="migration_guide.md" source="migration_guide.md">
## From Metabase

Migration is straightforward — both products use SQL queries with similar visualization layers. The Metabase JSON export can be parsed and re-imported via our API.

Steps:
1. Export Metabase questions and dashboards as JSON (Settings → Export)
2. Run `nw-metabase-importer dump.json --workspace ws_xyz`
3. Manually verify dashboards load correctly

About 80% of Metabase content imports automatically.
</doc>

<doc id="migration_guide.md" source="migration_guide.md">
## From homegrown / SQL files

If your "current platform" is a folder of SQL files plus screenshots:
1. Bulk-import SQL files via `nw-cli sql import ./queries`
2. Each file becomes a saved query, organized by folder
3. Build dashboards on top of queries in the UI
</doc>

details.question: What is the maximum file upload size on Business plan?
details.answer: The maximum single file upload size on the Business plan is 10 GB.
details.ground_truth: 10 GB
details.context:
<doc id="limits_and_quotas.md" source="limits_and_quotas.md">
## Other notable limits

- Maximum query response size: 100 MB inline (larger via Parquet)
- Maximum dashboard refresh frequency: 1 minute on Enterprise, 15 min on Business
- Maximum scheduled report frequency: every 15 minutes
- Maximum recipients per scheduled report: 50
- Audit log retention: 12 months (Business) / 7 years (Enterprise)
- Backup retention: 30 days (all paid plans)
</doc>

<doc id="limits_and_quotas.md" source="limits_and_quotas.md">
## Storage limits

| Limit | Free | Starter | Business | Enterprise |
|-------|------|---------|----------|------------|
| Total data storage | 5 GB | 50 GB | 500 GB | Custom |
| Single file upload | 100 MB | 1 GB | 10 GB | 10 GB |
| Total dashboards | 50 | 500 | 5000 | Unlimited |
| Charts per dashboard | 20 | 50 | 100 | 200 |
</doc>

<doc id="getting_started.md" source="getting_started.md">
For files (CSV, JSON, Parquet), use **Data → Upload** and drag-and-drop. Files up to 10 GB are supported.
</doc>

<doc id="regions_and_sla.md" source="regions_and_sla.md">
## SLA details

### Uptime targets
- **Business plan**: 99.9% monthly (43 minutes max downtime per month)
- **Enterprise plan**: 99.95% monthly (22 minutes max downtime per month)

### What's covered
- Web app availability (app.northwind.cloud)
- API availability (api.northwind.cloud)
- Query engine response time under documented limits
- Authentication services
</doc>

<doc id="faq.md" source="faq.md">
## Technical

**Q: What's the maximum query result size?**
A: 100K rows for inline display. Larger results are exported to Parquet/CSV and downloaded via signed URL.

**Q: Can I use the platform offline?**
A: No. Northwind is cloud-only. Edge / on-prem deployments are not supported.

**Q: How long does an initial sync take?**
A: Depends on data volume. A 100 GB warehouse typically syncs in 2-4 hours. Subsequent incremental syncs run in 1-15 minutes.
</doc>
